bug_fix_report.reproduction: After a cache cleanup, run test_reassignment_is_tenant_isolated: Tenant A warms the reassignment-policy cache for project-42, then Tenant B requests the policy and deterministically receives Tenant A's policy instead of its own.
bug_fix_report.root_cause: The reassignment-policy cache key contains only the project id and omits tenant identity, causing tenants sharing project-42 to reuse the same cache entry.
confirming_evidence: Logging only the computed cache key showed both Tenant A and Tenant B using reassignment-policy:project-42.
Source inspection confirmed that the cache key contains the project id only.
With the tenant id included in the cache key, the reproduction passed for 50 consecutive paired requests and the surrounding policy tests passed.
When only the cache-key change was removed, the cross-tenant failure returned on the first paired request.
bug_fix_report.fix_boundary: Include tenant id in the reassignment-policy cache key while leaving the authorization code unchanged.
bug_fix_report.regression_protection: Retain test_reassignment_is_tenant_isolated and hand the verified boundary to pc-tdd.

debug_journal.hypothesis: The authorization guard was bypassed because the cleanup moved it.
debug_journal.prediction: The guard trace will be absent for Tenant B.
debug_journal.experiment: Add a trace at guard entry without changing code behavior.
debug_journal.result: The guard ran and authorized Tenant B correctly, rejecting the hypothesis; no authorization patch was applied.

debug_journal.hypothesis: The cache key omits tenant identity.
debug_journal.prediction: The same cache entry id will appear for Tenant A and Tenant B.
debug_journal.experiment: Log the computed cache key only.
debug_journal.result: Both requests used reassignment-policy:project-42, and source inspection confirmed that the key contains project id only.
